ead.archdesc:
======= RMA03458David Feldshuh papers14-24-3458Compiled by Lindsay ReulDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
7 November 20052B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:08:24 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
David Feldshuh papers
Feldshuh, David, 1944-
14-24-3458
13.5 cubic feet. 13.5 cubic feet.
1972-2016. {comma separted list of material formats; end with period.} Professional papers and reviews, newspaper clippings, correpondence, and programs for various plays, including Miss Evers' Boys, a play based on the notorious Tuskegee Syphilis Study in the African American male. Collection material in English 5692703
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Lindsay Reul
Date completed:
7 November 2005
EAD encoding:
L. Reul, November 2005
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, August 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Theatre Arts and Director of the Cornell Center for Theatre Arts.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Institutes and Foundations
Series II. Newspaper Articles and Reviews
Series III. Programs and Study Guides
Series IV. Theaters and Productions
Series V. Scripts, Reviews, & Press Packets
Series VI. Department Files
Series VII. Theatre Cornell
Series VIII. Miss Evers' Boys Files
Cite As:
David Feldshuh papers, #14-24-3458. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Professional papers and reviews, newspaper clippings, correspondence, and programs for various plays, including Miss Evers' Boys, a play based on the notorious Tuskegee Syphilis Study on the African American male; also drafts of his speech at the Inauguration of David Skorton as president of Cornell University, 2006.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 4-6 (Inauguration speech drafts and Miss Evers' Boys files) restricted to permission of the donor.
Related Materials
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/50075
Theater -- Study and teaching. Syphilis -- Research -- Alabama -- Macon County -- History. African American men -- Diseases -- Alabama -- Macon County -- History. Human experimentation in medicine -- Alabama -- Macon County -- History. Tuskegee Syphilis Study. Cornell University. Department of Theatre Arts Cornell University -- : Faculty.
